meaning: adj.无变化的,不变的n.[数]不变式,不变量
examples: "Once we scale up, algorithms must be sensitive to tiny changes in identities and at the same time invariant to lighting, pose, age," Kemelmacher-Shlizerman said.
Step 1. Identify variant or invariant tasks.
Economists have been discussing this labeling problem and have been producing meaningful fiscal gap measures that are label-invariant, for 40 years.
The CDOI Approach contains no fixed techniques or invariant patterns in process and no causal theory about the concerns people bring.
